summary:
Claim: TSH assessment is necessary in galactorrhoea-amenorrhea syndromes even when serum prolactin is normal.  
Evidence: • Hypothyroidism reduces central dopamine tone, leading to disinhibited prolactin secretion despite normal serum levels. • Treatment of hypothyroidism often resolves galactorrhoea, confirming TSH’s diagnostic and therapeutic value.  
Answer: A
